rationale: Authority data for W40K-0171..W40K-0180 — the **modern Necromunda imprint relaunch cluster** of 2018-2021 Black-Library Necromunda titles, tied to the 2017 specialist-game-line re-release. Direct continuation of ssot-w40k-017's early/mid-2000s Necromunda cluster, now in modern-imprint register. Ten Necromunda-set entries: two Black-Library-Novella-Series entries (W40K-0171 *Wanted: Dead*, Mike Brooks 2018, Novella-Series-1-#6 — Jarene of the Escher Wild Cats; W40K-0174 *Low Lives*, Denny Flowers 2019, Novella-Series-2-#9 — Caleb Cursebound + Iktomi the ratskin in Hope's End); one mid-Kal-Jerico-revival novel (W40K-0172 *Sinner's Bounty*, Josh Reynolds 2019 — Reynolds takes over the Jerico baton from Rennie/McDermott, hunting Redemptionist Desolation Zoon); one Justin-D-Hill Escher-coming-of-age (W40K-0173 *Terminal Overkill*, 2019 — Brielle of the Wild Hydras vs Goliath Fettnir in the Dim Zone); one foundational modern-imprint anthology (W40K-0175 *Underhive: A Necromunda Anthology*, 2019 — collects the *Wanted: Dead* novella + nine shorts by Brooks/Harrison/Hinks/Reynolds/Haley/MacNiven/Kyme); one Mike-Brooks Cawdor-redemption novel (W40K-0176 *Road to Redemption*, 2020 — Zeke of House Cawdor, Floodgrave settlement); one Will-McDermott Mad-Donna-vs-Scrutinator-Servalen procedural (W40K-0177 *Soulless Fury*, 2020 — D'onne Ulanti + Scrutinator Primus Servalen with cyber-mastiff KB-88, House Cawdor antagonists); one mis-labelled-as-novel anthology (W40K-0178 *Uprising*, 2020 — actually a multi-author anthology: Annandale + Kloster + Hill + Flowers + Green + Brooks + McDermott + Wiltgren + Rath, framing-piece by Flowers); one Denny-Flowers Guild-of-Light vs Redemptionist novel (W40K-0179 *Fire Made Flesh*, 2021 — Tempes Sol of the Guild of Light vs Lord Silas Pureburn, the Fallen Dome of Periculus); and one Gary-Kloster ogryn-uprising novella (W40K-0180 *Spark of Revolution*, 2020, Novella-Series-3-#5 — Breaker Brass the ogryn, House of Chains / Goliath civil war). **Three format-compliance `data_conflict` flags raised**: W40K-0171 (SSOT `novel` → suggestion `novella`, Black Library Novella Series 1 #6), W40K-0174 (SSOT `novel` → suggestion `novella`, Black Library Novella Series 2 #9), W40K-0178 (SSOT `novel` → suggestion `anthology`, multi-author short-fiction collection per Amazon table-of-contents listing). **Modern-imprint authority surface forms** seeded: Caleb Cursebound + Iktomi (the Cursebound saga that runs W40K-0174 *Low Lives* → W40K-0178 *Uprising* framing piece → an unhandled-here later novel); Jarene + Wild Cats (Escher); Brielle + Wild Hydras + Fettnir + Red Tori + the Dim Zone (Hill's Escher coming-of-age); Desolation Zoon (Redemptionist antagonist of the modern-Kal-Jerico revival); Zeke + Floodgrave (Brooks' Cawdor redemption arc); Scrutinator Primus Servalen + KB-88 + Dog the ogryn (McDermott's Mad-Donna procedural revives the D'onne-Ulanti surface form first registered in ssot-w40k-017 W40K-0162 *Survival Instinct*); Tempes Sol + Guild of Light + Lord Silas Pureburn + the Fallen Dome of Periculus (Flowers' Periculus-arc); Breaker Brass + House of Chains (Kloster's ogryn-revolt). **Continuation of Necromunda factional vocabulary** from ssot-w40k-017: Houses Escher / Cawdor / Goliath / Orlock / Delaque / Van Saar / Helmawr / Ulanti continue; **first authority-layer Guild of Light + Corpse Guild + Spyrer / Venator surface forms** for completeness on Necromunda's Guild-clade taxonomy. **No `low_confidence` flags raised** — all ten have belastbare Lexicanum / Black-Library / Track-of-Words / Goodreads plot summaries; modern-imprint coverage is consistently better than the early/mid-2000s cluster. **Inquisition-consistency**: not triggered — no on-page Inquisitors in any of the ten; closest are Scrutinator Primus Servalen (Necromunda Enforcer rank, not Inquisition) and the Cawdor / Redemption religious-extortion register (which is Imperial-faith-adjacent but not Inquisitorial). **WebSearch: mean = 1.0 / book, max = 1 (over 10 books), 10 total searches** — one obligatory plot-confirmation search each, no books required follow-ups; modern-imprint coverage was uniformly good. **value_outside_vocabulary candidates** (none promoted): `bounty_hunter`, `ganger`, `noble-fallen-to-underhive`, `ogryn-protagonist`, `ratskin` (protagonist_class — Caleb Cursebound is a bounty-hunter/conman, Iktomi is a ratskin, Brielle is a ganger, Breaker Brass is an ogryn, Mad Donna is a fallen-noble; default workaround used is `civilian` since the catalog has no Necromunda-specific class); `novella` (format-field — Brief 061 omnibus-constraint covers anthology, but `novella` is its own SSOT-format-field value that the facet catalog `format` taxonomy doesn't separately model — only `book`); `anthology-mislabelled-as-novel` is structurally captured by the `data_conflict` flag rather than a vocabulary value. **Notable surface-form frequencies within this batch**: *Locations*: Necromunda ×10, Underhive ×10, Hive Primus ×9, The Spire ×2, Hope's End ×2 (W40K-0174 + W40K-0178 framing-piece), Floodgrave ×1, Dim Zone ×1, Periculus ×1; *Factions*: House Escher ×4 (Wild Cats + Wild Hydras), House Cawdor ×5 (W40K-0172 antagonist + W40K-0176 primary + W40K-0177 antagonist + W40K-0178 framing + W40K-0179 antagonist), Redemptionists ×5 (W40K-0172 + W40K-0174 + W40K-0177 + W40K-0178 + W40K-0179), House Goliath ×2 (W40K-0173 + W40K-0180), House Orlock ×2 (W40K-0174 antagonist + W40K-0178 venator-frame), House Helmawr ×3 (W40K-0172 + W40K-0177 Lord-Helmawr-dispatch + W40K-0180 framing), House Ulanti ×2 (W40K-0177 + Mad-Donna-callback-to-0162), Guilders ×6, Guild of Light ×1 (W40K-0179), Corpse Guild ×1 (W40K-0178 framing-piece), Necromunda Enforcers ×1 (W40K-0177 Scrutinator Servalen), Spyrers ×1, Imperium ×10; *Characters*: Caleb Cursebound ×2 (W40K-0174 + W40K-0178 framing-piece), Iktomi ×2 (W40K-0174 + W40K-0178 framing-piece), Kal Jerico ×1 (W40K-0172 — modern-imprint revival, separate from the 2005-2008 trilogy's Kal in ssot-w40k-017), Desolation Zoon ×1 (W40K-0172), Jarene ×1 (W40K-0171), Brielle ×1 (W40K-0173), Red Tori ×1 (W40K-0173 deceased mother), Fettnir ×1 (W40K-0173 antagonist), Zeke ×1 (W40K-0176), D'onne Ulanti / Mad Donna ×1 (W40K-0177 — revival of the W40K-0162 *Survival Instinct* surface form, fifteen years later), Scrutinator Primus Servalen ×1 (W40K-0177), KB-88 ×1 (W40K-0177 cyber-mastiff), Dog (the ogryn) ×1 (W40K-0177), Tempes Sol ×1 (W40K-0179), Lord Silas Pureburn ×1 (W40K-0179 antagonist), Breaker Brass ×1 (W40K-0180), Yar Umbra ×1 (W40K-0178 framing-piece venator). **Brief-071-Vertrag aktiv**: Override-Datei + Status-Log-Append in einem Commit ohne Co-Author-Trailer, keine sonstigen Datei-Edits. **Resolver-Pre-Note**: `cumulativeAfter=180`; **next natural Resolver-Pause-Schwelle ist bei `cumulativeBefore=200`** (`ssot-w40k-021`, W40K-0201..W40K-0210, in two iterations). Surface-form-pile since the last Resolver-Pass in 069 spans ssot-w40k-006..018 = 130 Bücher with the modern-Necromunda surface forms now stacked on top of the early/mid-2000s Necromunda cluster — Cowork-Empfehlung aus ssot-w40k-017 (Resolver-Pass vor cumulative=200 planen) gilt verstärkt: D'onne Ulanti appears in both ssot-w40k-017 (W40K-0162) and ssot-w40k-018 (W40K-0177) with a fifteen-year publication-gap, and the canonical-name choice (D'onne Ulanti vs Mad Donna vs Donna Ulanti vs D'onne) should be resolved before more modern-imprint Necromunda entries land.

overrides.synopsis: Jarene and the Wild Cats are House Escher fighters in Hive Primus, until an ambush leaves a Guilder and their own leader dead and turns the whole crew into outlawed prey. Hunted by Enforcers, guilders, rival Houses and bounty-hungry gangs, Jarene has to keep her sisters alive long enough to prove the killing was a set-up. Mike Brooks frames the chase as Underhive noir: a hunted-gang procedural about Escher loyalty, street justice and survival when Necromunda decides your name is worth more as a corpse.

flags.note: Black Library Novella Series 1 #6; SSOT-roster lists as novel

overrides.synopsis: Kal Jerico returns to Hive Primus chasing the richest bounty of his career: Desolation Zoon, a Redemptionist preacher tearing through the Underhive in a mining hauler loaded with stolen Guilder guns. The reward draws every serious Venator in the hive, turning the pursuit into a running gang-war chase with Kal at its smirking center. Built for readers meeting Kal fresh, it plays as modern Necromunda action-comedy, all banter, collapsing plans, rival hunters and bad decisions made at full hive speed.

Brielle grows up under the shadow of Red Tori, legendary leader of the Wild Hydras, until House Goliath overlord Fettnir kills her mother and breaks their House Escher territory in the Dim Zone. Cast alone into the black edges of Hive Primus, Brielle has to survive, harden, build a life from nothing and claw her way back toward vengeance. A grim coming-of-age revenge story told like a remembered Underhive memoir, where pain lingers for years before grief finally turns into a blade against Fettnir.

Caleb Cursebound, the Underhive's self-declared ninth-most-dangerous man, is hiding with Iktomi after bringing down a noble-house tyrant. Hope's End should be nowhere enough to disappear, but the mining settlement is trapped under a brutal House Orlock gang and desperate for a champion. Caleb promises more than he can safely give, while drink, paranoia and an old grudge close in around both fugitives. A rough-edged redemption novella about a braggart forced into heroism and hunted by the life he keeps outrunning.

flags.note: Black Library Novella Series 2 #9; SSOT-roster lists as novel

overrides.synopsis: Underhive collects Wanted: Dead and nine more modern Necromunda stories, using Hive Primus as a mosaic of vendettas, guild deals, zealot crusades and dirty survival. House Escher, Goliath, Orlock, Cawdor, Delaque and Van Saar all get dragged through Guilder caravans, Redemptionist fire, Spyrer hunts, mutant settlements and the grinding economy of violence beneath Necromunda's spires. Less a single plot than a foundational sampler of the relaunch-era setting, it moves through gang noir, black comedy, hive horror and street-level war, giving the reader a tour of a city where every bargain has teeth and every shortcut has a bill.

Zeke has left House Cawdor's faith-and-flagellant life behind, or convinced himself he has, until raiders burn Floodgrave and carry off the settlement's children into Hive Primus. To follow them, he straps on the weapons he swore away and walks back into the zealotry, brutality and Redemptionist logic that shaped him. With children's lives on the line, Mike Brooks turns the rescue into a grim road downhive, asking whether a man can use the old violence for one decent end without becoming its servant again in the dark.

D'onne Ulanti, still feared downhive as Mad Donna, tears through Hive Primus looking for a young captive taken by House Cawdor. Scrutinator Primus Servalen of the Necromunda Enforcers is sent after her with Lord Helmawr's seal and enough authority to open any door, but the trail is made of bodies, shell casings and Donna's impossible momentum. Dog, KB-88 and the Cawdor-held captive pull both women toward the same destination. A dual-POV manhunt thriller where rescue mission and law-enforcement pursuit collapse into one bloody corridor.

Uprising frames another sweep through modern Necromunda around Caleb Cursebound and a wider set of downhive rebellions, escapes and terrible bargains. Yar Umbra cuts a path through Hive Primus seeking a way off Necromunda; a House Cawdor juve finds a statue of the Emperor that can remake his life; a Corpse Guild drudge learns why some secrets must stay buried; and Caleb runs for Hope's End with enemies on his reputation as much as his heels. A multi-author anthology of hive unrest, faith, hunger and little revolutions, where the Underhive keeps turning private desperation into public violence and nobody leaves clean.

flags.note: Multi-author short-fiction collection (Annandale + Kloster + Hill + Flowers + Green + Brooks + McDermott + Wiltgren + Rath) per Amazon table-of-contents; SSOT-roster lists as novel

overrides.synopsis: The Fallen Dome of Periculus has been found again, and every noble, Guilder and hive-scum faction with a taste for profit wants what lies inside. Tempes Sol of the Guild of Light sees the dome as a weapon against Lord Silas Pureburn, a Redemptionist firebrand with the Underhive at his back, but the expeditions uncover more than loot: forbidden archeotech, flesh-hungry things and a rage that spreads through everyone it touches. Denny Flowers turns the treasure hunt into Necromunda archeotech horror, with religious violence burning under the skin.

Breaker Brass is an ogryn slave-labourer in the House of Chains, forging war machines for his House Goliath masters and crushing their enemies with steel fists. When civil war tears through the factorum, a rival gang spends him like scrap and leaves him broken. He rises instead with golden fists, new intelligence and a purpose that can become a servitor revolt. A brutal but strangely hopeful Necromunda novella about a discarded worker discovering the shape of freedom in the machinery built to own him.
